scope.summary: Make the default LlmClient's embedded model registry shared, enumerable, and labeled (UUIDv7 -> display_name/base_model_tag) so downstream pillars can name and list the local model, and give LoomSearchV2 a real embedding-model contract that degrades (not hard-errors) on dimension mismatch.
scope.origin: Deferred from MT-003 (2026-07-01) interconnectivity post-review. MT-003 wires the default LlmClient to the embedded ModelRuntime, but constructs a THROWAWAY single-entry ModelRegistry buried inside LocalRouter (llm/boot.rs) that is not shared, enumerable, or persisted, and whose UUIDv7 has no exposed display_name join. This blocks: MT-008 TraceProjection labeling the local model (groups by opaque UUIDv7), MT-012 operator model-picker enumeration, and Work Profiles provider_ref resolution (provider_id changed ollama->local_runtime). Separately, LoomSearchV2 hard-requires 768-dim embeddings (loom_search/mod.rs LOOM_SEARCH_EMBEDDING_DIM=768) and returns a HARD StorageError::Validation on mismatch, so configuring a normal chat model as the local default makes Loom reindex/search hard-error instead of degrade.
acceptance_criteria: The default LlmClient's embedded model registration is exposed through a shared/enumerable registry surface (UUIDv7 -> display_name/base_model_tag/runtime_binding) rather than a throwaway single-entry ModelRegistry hidden in LocalRouter; a backend API/lookup can enumerate and label the configured local model(s).
MT-008 TraceProjection (or the diagnostics projection) can resolve a local-model UUIDv7 to a human display name via this registry; a test proves the join.

The registry surface is consumable by the MT-012 operator model-picker (enumerate available local models) and by Work Profiles provider_ref resolution; a Work Profile that pinned provider_ref='ollama' resolves or is migrated deterministically (no silent dangle).

LoomSearchV2 has an explicit embedding-model contract: a dedicated embedding model / dimension adapter distinct from the chat model, and a dimension mismatch degrades to a typed non-fatal outcome (keyword/trigram fallback) instead of propagating a hard StorageError; a test proves reindex/search degrades rather than hard-errors on a non-768 embedding.
Same-change UserManual coverage for the shared registry surface + the LoomSearchV2 embedding-model contract; HBR-INT-009 posture recorded.
Pre- and post-implementation adversarial review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION; implementer does not self-certify COMPLETED.
proof_targets: TO_BE_FINALIZED_AT_ACTIVATION: exact Rust tests for (a) UUIDv7->display_name registry enumeration/label join (engine-free, --features test-utils), (b) LoomSearchV2 degrades (not hard-errors) on embedding-dimension mismatch, (c) Work Profiles provider_ref resolution/migration. Proof commands MUST name exact tests and fail on zero matching tests.
scope.risk_if_missed: The local model is unlabelable in diagnostics (MT-008) and unlistable in the operator picker (MT-012), Work Profiles pinned to the old ollama provider silently break, and configuring a local model can hard-crash LoomSearchV2 reindex on a dimension mismatch.

implementation_notes: MT-003 boot.rs build_default_local_client constructs ModelRegistry::default() with one entry inside LocalRouter; this MT makes that a shared/enumerable/labeled surface. Reconcile with the swarm's separate model_runtime registry usage (production_factory.rs) rather than creating a third disjoint registry.

LoomSearchV2 (loom_search/mod.rs, api/loom.rs refresh_loom_block_embedding) is the ONLY production caller of LlmClient::embedding(); FEMS/memory does not consume it. The 768-dim requirement + hard StorageError on mismatch is the crash surface.
Do NOT re-introduce Ollama as the embedding provider; the embedding model is another embedded ModelRuntime registration.
Rust-only; Rust backend + native egui/Argus where UI is touched.
This MT bridges MT-003 (default local client) to MT-008 (diagnostics labeling), MT-012 (picker enumeration), and Work Profiles.

pre_implementation_review.full_review_ref: session task aa63db63808f7a7b3 output (read-only adversarial, file:line evidence)
blockers_and_decisions: HIGH FACTUAL ERROR (F12): the 'reconcile with production_factory separate model_runtime registry world' premise is FALSE - production_factory only uses SandboxAdapterRegistry (in #[cfg(test)]) + imports RuntimeBinding; the ONLY production model_runtime::ModelRegistry instantiation is the throwaway at boot.rs. DECISION: drop the reconcile-with-production_factory directive; the task is to make boot's SINGLE registry shared, not unify nonexistent worlds.

HIGH SCAFFOLDING (F9): 'dedicated embedding model distinct from chat model' is materially larger than the degrade-fix and unsupported today (single-model config, no supports_embedding capability). DECISION: SPLIT AC-4 - 4a (embedding-dim degrade-not-error) stays in MT-014; 4b (dedicated embedding model + supports_embedding capability + second registration) DEFERRED to new MT-016 so it is not scaffolded here.

HIGH (F13): Work Profiles provider_ref has NO resolver today (free String, only non-empty check); 'resolves or is migrated' is net-new. DECISION: MT-014 adds a resolver validating against the canonical provider_id set (local_runtime, openai_compat), migrates legacy 'ollama' -> 'local_runtime', and emits a surfaced FR-EVT-PROFILE event (no silent in-place rewrite).

HIGH IDENTITY (F10): ModelId::new_v7() is re-minted every boot, so diagnostics grouped by model_id fragment across restarts. DECISION: registry surface MUST expose a stable cross-session anchor (artifact sha256 and/or base_model_tag) alongside the per-boot UUIDv7; MT-008 label-join defined against the stable anchor.

HIGH (F7/F8): the hard dim-mismatch error also 400s the SEARCH query path (not just reindex), and folding mismatch into NoModel is a SILENT drop (red_team risk). DECISION: add a distinct EmbedOutcome::DimMismatch{expected,actual}, emit a FR event on BOTH reindex and search, set semantic_available=false with a typed reason; assert event/reason in an engine-free test using InMemoryLlmClient::with_embedding_dim(896).

HIGH (F4): declared consumers MT-008/MT-012 do NOT depend on MT-014 and MT-014.blocks is empty. DECISION: downgrade 'feeds/breaks' language to 'provides an enumeration/label surface MT-008/MT-012 MAY adopt'; do not force rework of already-built MT-008.

HIGH (F3): durability posture unstated vs spec 4.6.5 'persisted registry row'. DECISION: registry is an in-memory shared handle re-derived deterministically from env config; the SELECTION decision (not the registry table) is recorded as an auditable EventLedger event per spec 4.3.9.4.4. No new Postgres registry table, no SQLite.

MED (F5/F6): registry unreachable through the object graph (private field in LocalRouter, no LlmClient accessor). DECISION: lift the shared registry into an AppState field / small enumeration API; define deterministic behavior for empty registry + unknown model_id (stable 'unknown model' sentinel label, never panic/blank).
MED (F14): built on MT-003 which is READY_FOR_VALIDATION (validator_verdict PENDING). Accepted risk; MT-003 regression guards are stable in code.
LOW (F11): enumeration keyed on model_id/sha256 (display_name is a non-unique label).

risks: A third disjoint registry world is created instead of unifying.
The dim-mismatch degrade silently drops semantic search without surfacing the misconfiguration.
Work Profiles migration silently rewrites operator intent.
minimum_controls: Reconcile with the existing model_runtime registry rather than adding a third.
Dim-mismatch degrade emits a typed, operator-visible warning/event, not a silent drop.
Work Profiles migration is deterministic and surfaced.

handshake_compatibility.rust_only_implementation: Rust backend plus native egui/Argus only.
handshake_compatibility.spec_realism_gate: Implementer may move only to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after real proofs; COMPLETED is validator-only.
